data.title: Why Is Arm Stock Falling Tuesday?
data.description:
The firm downgraded the stock to Equal-Weight from Overweight while lifting its price target to $150 from $135, signaling a more balanced risk-reward outlook.

Analyst Take

Morgan Stanley analyst Lee Simpson said Arm's push into chip development aligns with the rise of agentic AI but introduces execution risks in the near term.

He pointed to strong early progress, including talent acquisition and initial product delivery, but warned that slow commercial scaling and rising costs could limit upside. Simpson also flagged DRAM supply constraints and potential conflicts with existing licensees as factors that may cap valuation at $150.

Simpson described Arm's move into silicon as a structural shift tied to evolving AI workloads, noting that CPUs will remain central in coordinating increasingly autonomous systems. He added that Arm's AGI-focused CPU roadmap supports its long-term relevance and deeper ecosystem integration.

Growth Headwinds And Margin Pressure

However, he cautioned that chip revenue will take time to scale, while elevated research and engineering expenses could pressure margins in the interim. Demand softness and memory-related constraints may also weigh on growth through fiscal 2027.

The analyst warned that Arm's entry into chipmaking could create friction with licensing partners, as increased overlap may trigger customer pushback.

Simpson said the stock now reflects a sum-of-the-parts framework, applying a 30x EBIT multiple to the IP business and 15x to the chip segment. He outlined key risks to upside, including DRAM shortages, the outcome of Qualcomm-related litigation, and shifts in licensee strategies.

Financial Outlook And Long-Term Projections

Looking ahead, Simpson said clearer visibility into licensing growth and successful product launches could support sentiment. He expects chip revenue to reach about $1.2 billion by fiscal 2028 with 40% gross margins, though profitability may lag initially. Over time, margins could exceed 30% by fiscal 2031 as scale improves.

He projects total revenue of $9.28 billion and EPS of $4.16 in fiscal 2028, rising to $31 billion in revenue and $16.30 in EPS by fiscal 2031.

ARM Price Action: Arm Holdings shares were down 4.75% at $141.71 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: PepsiCo Earnings Seen Steady But Middle East Tensions, Snack Turnaround Take Center Stage
data.description:
The PepsiCo Analyst: Bank of America analyst Peter Galbo maintained a Neutral rating and price target of $173.

The PepsiCo Thesis: PepsiCo has operated in the UAE since 1962, distributing brands like Pepsi, Lay's, Aquafina and Quaker through local partners.

The company works with Dubai Refreshment Company and Abu Dhabi Refreshment Company, supported by five warehouses and two franchise manufacturing facilities across the Emirates.

Iranian strikes on the U.A.E. were reported as recently as last Tuesday, with the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh being hit by drones underscoring the broken diplomatic efforts between the U.S. and Iran.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Also at stake is the turnaround led by PepsiCo Foods North America (PFNA), Galbo said in the note.

NielsenIQ data indicate that PFNA "improved sequentially for a second consecutive quarter," accelerating from a 0.6% decline in the fourth quarter of 2024 to 0.4% growth in the first quarter, the analyst stated. This is in line with "our +0.5% organic sales forecast," he added.

The analyst reiterated the earnings estimates for the first quarter and full year 2026 at $1.53 per share and $8.60 per share, respectively.

Highlights of PepsiCo results for the fourth quarter of 2025:

  • Revenue: $29.34 billion, beating consensus estimate of $28.98 billion
  • Earnings: $2.26 per share, topping Street expectations of $2.24 per share
  • Organic revenue growth: 2.1%, driven by higher pricing and partially offset by a decline in volumes
  • Segment performance: Beverage unit volume grew 1%, while snacks volume fell 2%

PEP Price Action: Shares of PepsiCo had declined by 1.56% to $154.29 at the time of publication on Tuesday.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Stock Of The Day: A Bull Trap In Lumentum Holdings?
data.description:
Lumentum Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:LITE) is trading higher on Tuesday. It may be on the verge of a breakout.

• Lumentum Holdings stock is among today’s top performers.

But sometimes when a stock appears to have broken out, it reverses rapidly and gives back all of its gains. Traders call this a bull trap and they are looking to see if there is one in Lumentum. This is why it is the Stock of the Day.

Many times when a stock reaches a resistance level, it reverses and heads lower. This happens when some of the traders and investors who created the resistance with their sell orders become anxious and impatient.

They decide to reduce their offer prices to attract buyers. Other impatient and anxious sellers see this and do the same thing. It could result in a snowball effect that forces the shares into a downtrend.

Sometimes at resistance levels, buyers eventually overpower sellers and push the price higher. This is called a breakout.

Breakouts can be a bullish dynamic for a stock. It shows that the sellers who created the resistance have either finished or canceled their orders.

With these sellers gone, buyers will be forced to outbid each other if they wish to purchase shares. This can force the stock into an uptrend.

But sometimes it appears a stock may be breaking out, but it reverses and gives back all its gains. This is called a bull trap, or a false breakout.

These occur when sellers decide to step out of the market for a short period of time. They think that if they do so, the price will go higher and they can sell at a better price.

But when they come back into the market and start selling again, it can push the shares lower. This creates the bull trap.

The traps tend to be followed by downtrend. If Lumentum Holdings can't breakout, it may go lower.

Courtesy: Piotr Swat on Shutterstock
